meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code Skills to Scale Your SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Master Claude Code skills and Antigravity workflows to ship professional-grade SaaS products faster than ever. Beginner-level guide.
content.answer_block: Break through development plateaus by integrating Claude Code with Antigravity. This workflow automates boilerplate, enforces architectural best practices, and accelerates debugging. By treating your CLI as a senior pair programmer, you shift from writing basic scripts to deploying scalable, high-value client solutions in a fraction of the traditional development time.
content.intro:
The screen glows at 2 AM. You're staring at another 'basic' app, feeling the grind of repetitive boilerplate and the nagging doubt that your code isn't production-ready. You aren't just building an app; you’re fighting the friction of manual configuration and architectural indecision that keeps your freelance projects stuck in the 'junior' tier.

But the game has changed. By leveraging Claude Code as an autonomous engine and Antigravity as your structural framework, you can bypass the tedious grunt work that slows down every freelancer. It’s time to stop typing every line and start orchestrating high-performance systems. Let’s evolve your stack.

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Establish a professional root directory that enforces strict modularity from the first line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS project structure using a modular monorepo pattern. Include directories for core logic, API layers, and documentation.

steps.title: Architectural Blueprinting
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your database schema and API endpoints before writing a single line of application code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a subscription-based SaaS. Generate the Prisma schema and corresponding API types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate schema based on current requirements'

steps.title: Autonomous Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Offload the repetitive task of writing CRUD operations to Claude Code, ensuring consistency across your entire project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate optimized CRUD controllers for the user and billing entities, ensuring full TypeScript type-safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --write 'generate controllers for user and billing modules'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suites
steps.description: Never ship without validation. Automate unit tests for your core business logic using Claude's integrated analysis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Vitest unit tests for the core authentication and billing services generated in step 3.

steps.title: Refactoring & Optimization
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your codebase for performance bottlenecks and 'junior' code patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for performance bottlenecks, redundant logic, and non-optimized database queries.

pro_tips: Always define your 'Definition of Done' in a .claudeignore file to prevent the AI from refactoring stable production code.
Use Antigravity to generate custom CLI commands for your clients so they can manage their own data after you hand off the project.
Treat the Claude Code chat history as your project documentation; export it to Markdown to provide your clients with a 'Build Log'.
Enable 'Strict Mode' in your TypeScript config before running Claude Code to ensure the AI generates high-fidelity, production-grade code.
Use Antigravity to simulate edge-case user behavior to stress-test your application logic before the first beta release.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, begin by deploying a single feature module to a staging environment and run a smoke test. Once verified, use this as your master template for all future client projects.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop building basic apps and start shipping enterprise-level software at speed. The only thing missing is consistent implementation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
